one_line_ask: Clean and de-skew Apexguard's three AI-drafted PPF/tint logo rasters, trace them into crisp production SVG masters, derive one-color knockouts for vinyl weeding, then assemble a multi-size die-cut vinyl decal/sticker sheet by binding the vector marks and the approved-sizes CSV into the client's authored cut-line template and exporting a press-ready PDF + SVG.
brief: Apexguard Auto Film, a fictional performance-automotive brand selling paint protection film (PPF) and window tint, is rebranding and needs its new identity turned into production-ready vector artwork and a print-ready vinyl decal sheet for its shop glass, product boxes, and installer-kit labels. The client has drafted the concepts with an AI image tool and only has flat raster PNGs: a master wordmark lockup for 'Apexguard' (a clean modern condensed sans in matte black with a single deep-blue accent chevron, premium/minimalist, no gradients), a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard SHIELD' (the PPF line — the wordmark plus a simple hex-shield glyph), and a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard TINT' (the window-tint line — the wordmark plus a tint-bar glyph rendered as flat bands). All three rasters carry the usual AI-export problems: soft anti-aliased edges, a faint off-white textured card behind the art, mild luminance noise, and each is tilted a couple of degrees off level. They cannot go to a vinyl cutter/printer as-is because they do not scale, have no clean cut path, and have no transparency. The client also supplies (a) an authored die-cut decal-sheet template (decal_sheet_template.ai) laid
